source_rationale: Nominal value anchored to DD analyses for J. lybica; exact 10–12 °C range must be verified in cited sources [34,13,19].
local_analysis: Semi-elasticity per +1 degC; do not use percent elasticity in Celsius.
notes: Thermal-location parameter; coupled to all mortality stress minima through derived T_opt.

source_rationale: Nominal upper activity bound anchored to [34]; exact 33–37 °C range must be verified.
local_analysis: Semi-elasticity per +1 degC; do not use percent elasticity in Celsius.
notes: Thermal-location parameter; coupled to all mortality stress minima through derived T_opt.

propagation_rule: Updated whenever T_min or T_max changes.
range_analysis: Inherited through T_min and T_max scenarios.
notes: Do not include as an independent parameter in rankings.

source_rationale: Effective aggregate oviposition coefficient per TOTAL adult. The nominal value 5 is retained from the original case-study parameterization and is informed only by the scale of published per-female fecundity values for J. lybica and related Empoasca [34,37,38]; it is not obtained by applying a sex-ratio correction because the reproductive female fraction is not identifiable from the available trap data. The explored 3-6 interval is therefore literature-anchored but exploratory for the effective per-total-adult coefficient.
local_analysis: Normalized local elasticity using a small symmetric step (e.g. ±1%).
notes: A is total adult density. beta_max is a model-scale effective coefficient, partly confounded with A_intro during early oviposition; no sex-specific quantitative claim is made.
Bounds represent assumed, not estimated, uncertainty.

range_analysis: Low/nominal/high; E,N,A and ENEI trajectories; timing shifts.

source_rationale: Not directly measured in Mazzoni et al. (2003) [40], which reports adult captures per sticky trap rather than adults per leaf. Total-adult immigration pulse selected to reflect early-season colonization; Appendix A.3.1 indicates strong amplification.
propagation_rule: Pulse applied on biofix date defined by T_move.
local_analysis: Normalized local elasticity (small step), with caution about multiplicative confounding.
range_analysis: Use full 0.01-0.04 span; report timing and amplitude effects.
notes: Internal modelling convention, not a direct field estimate. Factor-of-two exploratory span around the nominal. Confounded with beta_eff during the early reproductive phase (only the product beta_eff*A_intro drives early oviposition).

source_rationale: Exploratory biofix threshold. Mazzoni et al. (2003) [40] reports the first sticky-trap appearance of J. lybica in the first decade of July, whereas the current T_move=22 °C rule triggers the model pulse on 14 June 2002; the 20–24 °C range is therefore used to test colonization-timing uncertainty rather than as a literature-estimated thermal interval.
propagation_rule: Recompute the first daily crossing date for every scenario.
local_analysis: Scenario-only; exclude from differential elasticity because the event date is discrete/piecewise constant.
range_analysis: Report biofix-date, warning-date and lead-time shifts in days.
notes: The nominal thermal biofix precedes first species-specific trap detection by about three weeks. Treat T_move as a discrete scenario parameter and discuss the difference between physiological movement suitability and observable trap appearance.
